input.prompt: Look up the Claude Code changelog for versions 1.0.89, 1.0.90, and 1.0.91 (or whatever the exact version numbering is). The user wants to know what new commands or slash commands were introduced in those versions. Search the web or official docs for the release notes or changelog. Report back concisely what new commands/features were added in each of those versions.

content.text:
Based on my search, I have some important findings:

## Version Numbers

The Claude Code version numbering you're looking for (1.0.89, 1.0.90, 1.0.91) appears to be outdated. **Claude Code is currently at version 2.1.x** (as of April 2026), with the latest being **2.1.91**.

If you're asking about versions from the 2.x series instead, I can provide details on those. However, the exact versions 1.0.89-1.0.91 may be quite old or may not exist in the public release history.

## Recent Slash Commands Added (2.1.x releases)

From the current 2.x releases, notable new commands include:

- **/effort** — Set model effort level
- **/loop** — Run prompts or commands on recurring intervals
- **/color** — Set prompt-bar color for sessions
- **/powerup** — Interactive lessons with animated demos
- **/simplify** and **/batch** — Bundled slash commands
- **/remote-control** — Bridge sessions between Claude Code and claude.ai/code

## Where to Find Full Changelog

For the complete and authoritative changelog, check:

- [Official Claude Code Changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog.md)
- [GitHub Repository CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

Can you clarify which versions you need? If you meant 2.1.89, 2.1.90, and 2.1.91 instead, I can dig into the specific features added in those releases.

Sources:
- [Claude Code Changelog - Official Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog.md)
- [GitHub Claude Code Repository](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases)

input.description: Fetch Claude Code changelog for versions 2.1.89-2.1.91
input.prompt: Fetch the official Claude Code changelog and find what new commands or features were introduced specifically in versions 2.1.89, 2.1.90, and 2.1.91. Try fetching from https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md or the npm package changelog. Report only the relevant entries for those three versions, focusing on new commands, slash commands, or notable features.

content.text:
Based on the official Claude Code changelog, here are the key new commands and features introduced in versions 2.1.89, 2.1.90, and 2.1.91:

## Version 2.1.91
**New Commands/Features:**
- **MCP Tool Result Persistence Override** — Added `_meta["anthropic/maxResultSizeChars"]` annotation allowing results up to 500K characters without truncation (useful for large database schemas)
- **Shell Execution Control** — Added `disableSkillShellExecution` setting to disable inline shell execution in skills, custom slash commands, and plugin commands
- **Deep Link Multi-line Support** — Deep links now support up to 5,000 characters with encoded newlines (`%0A`)
- **Plugin Executables** — Plugins can now ship executables under `bin/` and invoke them as bare commands from the Bash tool

## Version 2.1.90
**New Commands/Features:**
- **`/powerup`** — Interactive lessons teaching Claude Code features with animated demonstrations
- **Protected Directories** — Added `.husky` to protected directories in acceptEdits mode
- **Environment Variable** — Added `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_KEEP_MARKETPLACE_ON_FAILURE` to keep existing marketplace cache when `git pull` fails

## Version 2.1.89
**New Commands/Features:**
- **`/powerup`** — Interactive lessons with animated demos (same command mentioned in 2.1.90)
- **`PermissionDenied` Hook** — Fires after auto mode classifier denials; return `{retry: true}` to allow retry
- **Named Subagents** — Added named subagents to `@` mention typeahead suggestions
- **`"defer"` Permission Decision** — Added for `PreToolUse` hooks in headless sessions
- **MCP Non-blocking Connection** — `MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=true` environment variable to skip MCP connection wait in `-p` mode
- **Flicker-free Rendering** — Added `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` environment variable for virtualized scrollback

The most significant additions across these three versions are the `/powerup` command (interactive lessons), improved plugin capabilities with executables and shell control, and enhanced MCP handling for non-blocking connections and larger results.

content.text:
Based on the official Claude Code changelog, here are the key new commands and features introduced in versions 2.1.89, 2.1.90, and 2.1.91:

## Version 2.1.91
**New Commands/Features:**
- **MCP Tool Result Persistence Override** — Added `_meta["anthropic/maxResultSizeChars"]` annotation allowing results up to 500K characters without truncation (useful for large database schemas)
- **Shell Execution Control** — Added `disableSkillShellExecution` setting to disable inline shell execution in skills, custom slash commands, and plugin commands
- **Deep Link Multi-line Support** — Deep links now support up to 5,000 characters with encoded newlines (`%0A`)
- **Plugin Executables** — Plugins can now ship executables under `bin/` and invoke them as bare commands from the Bash tool

## Version 2.1.90
**New Commands/Features:**
- **`/powerup`** — Interactive lessons teaching Claude Code features with animated demonstrations
- **Protected Directories** — Added `.husky` to protected directories in acceptEdits mode
- **Environment Variable** — Added `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_KEEP_MARKETPLACE_ON_FAILURE` to keep existing marketplace cache when `git pull` fails

## Version 2.1.89
**New Commands/Features:**
- **`/powerup`** — Interactive lessons with animated demos (same command mentioned in 2.1.90)
- **`PermissionDenied` Hook** — Fires after auto mode classifier denials; return `{retry: true}` to allow retry
- **Named Subagents** — Added named subagents to `@` mention typeahead suggestions
- **`"defer"` Permission Decision** — Added for `PreToolUse` hooks in headless sessions
- **MCP Non-blocking Connection** — `MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=true` environment variable to skip MCP connection wait in `-p` mode
- **Flicker-free Rendering** — Added `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` environment variable for virtualized scrollback

The most significant additions across these three versions are the `/powerup` command (interactive lessons), improved plugin capabilities with executables and shell control, and enhanced MCP handling for non-blocking connections and larger results.

Here's what was added in Claude Code 2.1.89–2.1.91:

---

### 2.1.91
- **Plugin executables** — plugins can ship binaries under `bin/` and invoke them directly from Bash
- `disableSkillShellExecution` setting — blocks inline shell execution in skills/slash commands
- MCP tool results up to 500K chars via `_meta["anthropic/maxResultSizeChars"]`
- Deep links now support up to 5,000 characters with encoded newlines

### 2.1.90
- **`/powerup`** — interactive lessons with animated demos for learning Claude Code features
- `.husky` added to protected directories in acceptEdits mode
- `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_KEEP_MARKETPLACE_ON_FAILURE` env var for resilient plugin caching

### 2.1.89
- **`PermissionDenied` hook** — fires after auto-mode denials; return `{retry: true}` to allow retry
- **Named subagents** in `@` mention typeahead
- **`"defer"` permission decision** for `PreToolUse` hooks in headless sessions
- `MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=true` — skip MCP wait in `-p` mode
- `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` — flicker-free rendering for virtualized terminals

The biggest additions: `/powerup` command and the `PermissionDenied` hook.
can I run the powerup slash command

content.thinking: The user wants to know if they can run the /powerup slash command. Let me check if it's available as a skill or built-in command.

content.text:
Yes — just type `/powerup` in the Claude Code chat. It's a built-in slash command (not a skill), so it runs directly without any setup.

It'll launch an interactive lesson with animated demos teaching Claude Code features.
